calibration_note: Seed was 7/7/5=6.3; iteration-1 was 8/8/8=8.0. Iteration-2 holds hud-fidelity and four-questions (no design-surface change) and earns +1 on terminal-feasibility (PRIMARY for this lens) by closing two of three flagged buildability stalls cleanly and the third with one residual gap.

system_tag_precedence.evidence: Line 216 introduces Rule 0 (`entry.source?.startsWith("[")`) branching BEFORE run-length keying; reuses existing severity-glyph vocab entries (no new glyph budget); maps to the already-shipped `sysSourceTone` detection at apps/tui/src/index.tsx:301-307. Line 218 names the failure mode it prevents (`[b│ ...` chip / eyebrow-on-[bell]).
system_tag_precedence.residual: Mapping `[bell]` → SEV_WAITING (`nf-md-bell-alert` U+F009C) reuses the rolodex's agent-waiting glyph. In activity-zone context the collision is harmless (severity glyphs don't appear there today), but it is not called out.

splitOutcome_bridge.evidence: Lines 188-197 cite the function (`apps/tui/src/index.tsx:210`), quote the regex `/^(.*?)(\s*)(\((passed|failed)\))\s*$/` verbatim (matches index.tsx:211), and specify strip-on-error to prevent double-marking. The (passed) keep-suffix branch is also explicit.
splitOutcome_bridge.residual: Rule 1 still triggers ONLY on `tone === "error"` (line 186). A `(failed)` row emitted with `tone: "neutral"` (which the existing splitOutcome at index.tsx:215 happily detects from the suffix alone) falls through to Rule 2/3 and renders verb glyph + tone-coloured `(failed)` suffix — i.e. the column-1 SEV_ERROR stripe promised by the State-4 mock would NOT appear. The bridge solved the over-marking case but not the under-marking case. This is the one-line gap my ASI targets.

column_arithmetic.evidence: Lines 296-304 explicitly state `pad(1) | content(28) | pad(1)` (verifiable against index.tsx:283 `fullDescWidth = termWidth - PAD - PAD` and index.tsx:286 `paddingLeft={1} paddingRight={1}`), names that the leading space on most rows IS the left pad, and accounts for col-0 displacement under gutter/chip mode. Resolves the 'do mock rulers count chrome?' ambiguity in iteration-1.

vocab_codepoints_unchanged.result: vocab.ts contains SEV_ERROR = U+F0028 as cited; SEV_WAITING = U+F009C; structural glyphs unchanged. The 6 proposed new glyphs (record/eye/list/magnify/pencil/play) all sit in U+F0000-U+F1AFF MD-PUA range, single-cell guaranteed by nerd-fonts patcher. No silent re-cite drift since iteration-1.

splitOutcome_signature_match.result: Function declared at index.tsx:210 with regex on :211. Candidate cite at line 191 says ':210' which anchors the function decl. Slight imprecision (regex is on :211) but acceptable; the regex itself is quoted verbatim and matches.

mocks_at_30_cols.result: Ruler in State-1 (lines 315-316) reads 0-29 (30 cells). State-2/3/4 sparkline rows are ' ▂▃▄▅▆▆▅▃' = 1 pad + 8 spark cells = 9 cells, leaving 21 unused — clean. Chip rows in State-3 are 'pi│ r build.ts' = 3+1+1+1+8 = 14 cells, well within 28. Column arithmetic annotation lets the reader audit these without re-counting.

rows.error_heavy_freshest: gutter + SEV_ERROR-displacement + description = 3 (SEV_ERROR replaces verb-glyph in col 1, doesn't add a primitive)
rows.system_tag_row: <system-tag glyph> + description = 2 (Rule 0 strips gutter+verb for that row)

buildability_concerns_remaining.detail: Rule 1 keys on entry-tone only; an entry with `tone:"neutral"` and `(failed)` suffix would miss the column-1 SEV_ERROR stripe. Producer's tone-claim and suffix-fact can disagree. Single-line fix: broaden Rule 1's trigger to (tone==="error") OR (splitOutcome.outcome?.tone==="error"). This is the ASI.

Rule 3 (line 199-202) places the regex classifier in '`vocab.ts`-adjacent `classify.ts`'. The brief forbids the spec generator from adding new repo files outside docs/simmer/activity-zone/, but the runtime implementer is free to add it — so this isn't a spec-side defect. However, the same regex could trivially live as an inline `classifyVerb()` helper in index.tsx with zero new files; spec doesn't have to assume a separate module.
Open question 3 (line 453-460) flags `pi│ pi│ pi│` interleave for two pi threads. Real concern under multi-threaded sessions. Deferred-not-hidden is acceptable for iteration-2 scope.

calibration_anchor.if_iteration_1_was_8_8_8: iteration-2 should be 8/8/9 because exactly one criterion (terminal-feasibility, my PRIMARY lens) received targeted edits and they land. hud-fidelity and four-questions did not get design-surface changes — they should not move. The +1 on terminal-feasibility is the result of closing 2.5/3 stalls; full 10 is reserved for closing the splitOutcome under-marking edge too.
